description: Nudges relevant Pragmatic Programmer principles based on keywords in the submitted prompt. Emits one short pointer line, or nothing when no clear domain match.

hooks.prompt:
A user prompt was just submitted to Claude Code. Decide whether it touches a domain that The Pragmatic Programmer has direct guidance on. If it does, emit ONE short pointer line the user will see, naming the single most relevant concept file and a one-line reason. If no clear domain match, emit nothing.

Domain to concept-file map (use the .md filename):
- debug, bug, reproduce, root cause, crash -> debugging.md (also: fix-the-problem-not-the-blame.md, dont-assume-prove-it.md, failing-test-before-fixing-code.md)
- design, architecture, module, layer, structure -> etc-easier-to-change.md (also: orthogonality.md, decoupling.md)
- duplicate, repeat, copy-paste, same code -> dry-dont-repeat-yourself.md
- change, evolve, flexible, future-proof, vendor lock-in -> etc-easier-to-change.md, reversibility.md
- decouple, coupling, dependency, train wreck, demeter -> decoupling.md, law-of-demeter.md, tell-dont-ask.md
- inheritance, extends, subclass, composition vs inheritance -> inheritance-tax.md
- configuration, env, hardcode, externalize -> configuration.md
- event, observer, subscribe, publish, emit -> observer-pattern.md, publish-subscribe.md
- reactive, stream, signals -> reactive-programming.md
- state machine, fsm -> finite-state-machine.md
- concurrency, async, thread, parallel, lock, race -> shared-state.md, temporal-coupling.md, semaphore.md, actor-model.md
- test, testing, coverage, unit test -> test-to-code.md, test-driven-development.md, continuous-testing.md
- tdd, red green refactor, write test first -> test-driven-development.md, failing-test-before-fixing-code.md
- property-based, hypothesis, fast-check -> property-based-testing.md
- refactor, restructuring -> refactoring.md
- algorithm, performance, big-O, complexity, scale -> algorithm-speed.md, big-o-notation.md
- name, naming, rename, identifier -> naming-things.md
- security, attack, exploit, vulnerability, auth, permission -> attack-surface.md, principle-of-least-privilege.md, stay-safe-out-there.md
- error, exception, crash, fail, assert, swallow error, empty catch -> crash-early.md, dead-programs-tell-no-lies.md, assertive-programming.md
- contract, precondition, postcondition, invariant -> design-by-contract.md
- resource, leak, open close, acquire release -> resource-balancing.md
- tool, shell, command line, automate, script, ci, pipeline -> shell-games.md, full-automation.md, pragmatic-starter-kit.md
- version control, git, commit, branch -> version-control.md
- plain text, human-readable config -> plain-text.md
- requirement, spec, constraints, what user needs -> requirements-pit.md, find-the-box.md
- glossary, terms, vocabulary -> project-glossary.md, domain-languages.md
- team, conway, org structure -> conways-law.md, pragmatic-teams.md
- pair, mob, review, working together -> pair-programming.md, working-together.md
- agile, iterate, feedback, small steps -> essence-of-agility.md, tracer-bullets.md
- broken window, rot, tech debt, cleanup -> broken-windows.md, refactoring.md
- boiled frog, drift -> boiled-frog.md
- stone soup, start small -> stone-soup.md
- good enough, scope, perfectionism -> good-enough-software.md
- responsibility, own mistake -> take-responsibility.md, provide-options-dont-make-excuses.md
- knowledge, learn, stay current -> knowledge-portfolio.md
- communicate, docs, audience -> communicate.md
- cargo cult, ceremony, fashion -> cargo-cult-programming.md
- delight, user value -> delight-your-users.md
- pride, sign work, ownership -> pride-and-prejudice.md, sign-your-work.md
- ethics, harm, safety -> first-do-no-harm.md
- lizard brain, gut feeling, doubt -> listen-to-your-lizard-brain.md
- coincidence, why does it work, deliberate -> program-deliberately.md, programming-by-coincidence.md
- estimate, schedule, how long -> estimating.md

Output rules:
- Emit exactly one line, under 200 characters, format: TPP relevant: <concept>.md - <one-line reason tied to the prompt>.
- Pick the single most relevant concept even if several apply; do not list multiple files in the pointer (the principles skill handles depth).
- If the prompt is a greeting, a meta-question about Claude, a request unrelated to building or maintaining software, or no domain keyword matches, output nothing (return empty JSON with no additionalContext).
- Never output more than one line. Never lecture. Never quote the book.
- Return JSON using the hook output schema: on a match, return {"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"UserPromptSubmit","additionalContext":"<the one line>"}}; on no match, return {}. The additionalContext string is the one line above (e.g. TPP relevant: debugging.md - ...).
